prompt: Design a Offline Operations Suite for Workplace Nutrition and Resource Management to unify on-device meeting reservations, meal/kit ordering, and employee learning in a single high-performance Android app. Regular Users browse a room/equipment calendar, submit meeting requests, add attendees, and attach agendas; Supervisors approve/deny requests and can require check-in within a 10‑minute window around the start time (otherwise the reservation is marked “no-show”). Companions can act on behalf of a user for booking and ordering when delegated, but cannot approve or issue refunds. Admins manage resources, business rules, and access policies. The UI is built with Android Views optimized for speed: resource and order lists render via RecyclerView + DiffUtil to sustain 60fps scrolling with 5,000+ rows, and attachments/photos (e.g., signed delivery slips) are downsampled with an LRU cache keeping peak image memory under 20MB. Booking screens detect conflicts instantly and auto-recommend up to three available time slots within the next 14 days, honoring buffer rules (e.g., 10 minutes setup/cleanup). Ordering supports a shopping cart, order placement, invoice details, notes/tags, split/merge for partial fulfillment, delivery confirmation, and return/exchange/refund workflows; payments are recorded offline (cash, internal wallet balance, or “external tender recorded”) and users can print/share a local receipt PDF. Attendee notifications and order reminders use on-device scheduled notifications only, with quiet hours defaulting to 9:00 PM–7:00 AM. All processing is fully offline with no server-side logic; business logic resides in ViewModel/UseCase layers, persisted locally using Room (SQLite) with Koin for dependency injection. Authentication is local username + password (minimum 10 characters, at least one number), with a 5-attempt lockout for 15 minutes; sessions auto-expire after 30 minutes idle, and optional device binding limits accounts to 2 devices unless an Admin resets the binding locally. Fine-grained RBAC/ABAC is enforced per feature, record, and field (e.g., only Supervisors/Admins can view attendee lists; invoice tax fields are Admin-only), with sensitive fields encrypted at rest and masked in UI logs. Orders run a strict state machine in a single Room transaction per transition; unpaid orders marked “pending tender” auto-cancel after 30 minutes, restocking inventory immediately. Data governance is implemented through a local metadata/taxonomy and validation rules (e.g., price must be $0.01–$9,999.99; missing allergen flags are blocked), with anomaly alerts and rule-hit metrics stored for offline analytics. A configuration/data-dictionary and form engine supports versioning and canary rollout by role or device group on the same device, while reconciliation closes daily with configurable settlement cycles (e.g., weekly on Friday 6:00 PM) and discrepancy tickets for manual review. Composite indexes (e.g., resourceId+startTime, orderStatus+createdAt) keep common queries under 50ms on 100,000 records, and all IO/computation runs off the main thread to prevent ANR under Android 10+ scoped storage and background restrictions.
